name: Object-Oriented Programming
description: Master classes, objects, inheritance, polymorphism, and encapsulation in Python.

lessons.title: Classes and Objects
lessons.description: Create classes to model real-world entities, instantiate objects, and understand the difference between class and instance attributes.
keyPoints: Classes are blueprints for creating objects with attributes and methods
The __init__ method is the constructor that initializes instance attributes
Instance attributes belong to individual objects; class attributes are shared across all instances
Methods are functions defined within a class that operate on instances
Self parameter refers to the current instance being operated on
lessons.example:
class BankAccount:
    """Simple bank account class"""
    
    # Class attribute (shared across all instances)
    bank_name = "Neopy Bank"
    account_counter = 0
    
    def __init__(self, owner, initial_balance=0):
        """Constructor - initializes new account"""
        # Instance attributes
        self.owner = owner
        self.balance = initial_balance
        self._account_number = self._generate_account_number()
        BankAccount.account_counter += 1
    
    def _generate_account_number(self):
        """Private method to generate account number"""
        return f"ACC{BankAccount.account_counter + 1:06d}"
    
    def deposit(self, amount):
        """Deposit money into account"""
        if amount > 0:
            self.balance += amount
            return True
        return False
    
    def withdraw(self, amount):
        """Withdraw money from account"""
        if 0 < amount <= self.balance:
            self.balance -= amount
            return True
        return False
    
    def get_balance(self):
        """Get current balance"""
        return self.balance
    
    def __str__(self):
        """String representation"""
        return f"Account({self.owner}, Balance: ${self.balance})"

# Creating objects
account1 = BankAccount("Alice", 1000)
account2 = BankAccount("Bob", 500)

# Using methods
account1.deposit(500)
account1.withdraw(200)
print(account1.get_balance())  # 1300
print(account1)  # Account(Alice, Balance: $1300)

# Accessing class attributes
print(BankAccount.bank_name)  # Neopy Bank
print(f"Total accounts: {BankAccount.account_counter}")  # Total accounts: 2

# Instance vs class attributes
print(account1.bank_name)  # Neopy Bank (inherited from class)
account1.bank_name = "Custom Bank"  # Creates instance attribute
print(account1.bank_name)  # Custom Bank
print(account2.bank_name)  # Neopy Bank (still class attribute)

lessons.title: Inheritance and Polymorphism
lessons.description: Create class hierarchies using inheritance, override methods, and leverage polymorphism for flexible code design.
keyPoints: Inheritance allows a class to inherit attributes and methods from a parent class
super() calls parent class methods and enables proper method resolution
Method overriding lets subclasses provide specific implementations of parent methods
Polymorphism enables treating objects of different classes through a common interface
Multiple inheritance allows a class to inherit from multiple parent classes
Abstract base classes (ABC) define interfaces that subclasses must implement

lessons.title: Encapsulation and Properties
lessons.description: Control access to class attributes using encapsulation, name mangling, and property decorators.
keyPoints: Encapsulation bundles data and methods while controlling access to internal state
Single underscore prefix (_var) indicates protected attribute by convention
Double underscore prefix (__var) triggers name mangling for pseudo-privacy
@property decorator creates getter methods that behave like attributes
@attribute.setter enables validation logic when setting attribute values
@attribute.deleter defines deletion behavior
Property decorators maintain a clean interface while adding validation logic

lessons.title: Magic Methods (Dunder Methods)
lessons.description: Implement special methods that define how objects behave with built-in operations like arithmetic, comparisons, and container protocols.
keyPoints: Magic methods are special methods with double underscores (__method__)
__init__ is the constructor, __str__ and __repr__ control string representation
Arithmetic methods (__add__, __sub__, __mul__) enable operator overloading
Comparison methods (__eq__, __lt__, __gt__) enable rich comparisons
Container methods (__len__, __getitem__, __setitem__) make objects behave like sequences
Context manager methods (__enter__, __exit__) enable 'with' statement support
